review.required_specialty: board-certified dermatologist or jurisdictionally equivalent independent dermatologist
review.blocking_reason: No independent clinical review has been recorded. This proposal must not be loaded by production code.

interpretation.detector_output_is: image-feature evidence for a named model label
interpretation.detector_output_is_not: a diagnosis, severity assessment, malignancy screen, or treatment authorization
interpretation.confidence_is: model evidence quality for the predicted label
interpretation.confidence_is_not: probability that the user has a clinical diagnosis
interpretation.required_finding_prefix: The image model found features most consistent with
interpretation.diagnostic_confirmation: A clinician may need history, palpation, dermoscopy, Wood lamp examination, or biopsy; a photograph cannot supply those checks.

global_rules.rule: Until review.approved is true and the approved report hash and policy version match the deployed artifacts, return findings and safety-oriented referral wording only; do not generate an active treatment routine.
A missing required or label-conditional intake answer is not a favorable default.
Select a care path by the exact detector label. Do not collapse labels into acne, scarring, or pigmentation groups for treatment selection.
Return at most one product for each requested routine role within an AM or PM session. An empty eligible set remains empty.
Do not substitute a different ingredient, role, exposure, strength, or formulation when the exact proposed target has no eligible product.
Prescription drugs and procedures may be described as clinician discussion options only. The application must not instruct a user to start, stop, obtain, or change one.

verified ranks above partial above unverified, but incomplete non-safety evidence alone is not a universal exclusion. Exact active identity and any fact required by a safety or role gate remain mandatory.
Never match a mole-removal product or an unsupervised high-strength chemical peel.

intake_contract.unknown_behavior: defer the affected active-treatment path and explain which answer is needed

required: authoritative evidence for active identity, strength, exposure, cadence, and contraindications; a regulatory Drug Facts or prescription label is mandatory only when the reviewed therapeutic intent claims OTC or prescription drug status
no stated intended area that excludes the face; unknown or empty intended area lowers completeness but remains eligible
known format, when present, must be permitted; unknown format lowers completeness but remains eligible
leave-on exposure unless the reviewed path explicitly permits rinse-off
SPF 30 or greater from an authoritative source
no stated intended area that excludes the face; unknown or empty intended area lowers completeness but remains eligible
known format, when present, must be permitted; unknown format lowers completeness but remains eligible
melasma_additional_required: deterministically parsed iron oxide or CI 77491, CI 77492, or CI 77499 ingredient

required: dedicated scar-care evidence role; this does not add a routine role to the current closed vocabulary
exact silicone sheet or silicone gel formulation evidence

labels.finding_wording: The image model found features most consistent with a closed comedo (a white or skin-colored plugged pore).
labels.finding_limit: A photograph cannot confirm that the bump is acne or exclude lookalikes such as milia, folliculitis, keratosis pilaris, or perioral dermatitis.

retail_target_actives.cadence: per Drug Facts; introductory cadence wording requires clinical approval
retail_target_actives.eligibility: age 12 or older; exclude pregnant or trying; defer nursing or unknown pregnancy status for clinician review

retail_target_actives.formulation: FDA-compliant OTC acne drug with exact exposure and directions
retail_target_actives.eligibility: no allergy, conflicting current active, or product contraindication

clinician_options: topical retinoid or combination therapy when OTC care is unsuitable or unsuccessful
azelaic acid when clinically appropriate
care_path.review_period: Review response and tolerability at 12 weeks; improvement may not be visible for 6-8 weeks.
stop_or_escalate: severe irritation or allergic reaction
diagnostic uncertainty
painful deep lesions, scarring, or persistent pigment change
no adequate response after a completed reviewed course
prohibited_matches: unverified cosmetic retinol substituted for adapalene
scrub or peel substituted for a leave-on treatment

labels.finding_wording: The image model found features most consistent with an open comedo (a blackhead caused by an open plugged pore, not trapped dirt).
labels.finding_limit: A photograph cannot confirm acne or distinguish every pigmented or follicular lookalike.

retail_target_actives.cadence: per Drug Facts; introductory cadence wording requires clinical approval
retail_target_actives.eligibility: age 12 or older; exclude pregnant or trying; defer nursing or unknown pregnancy status for clinician review

retail_target_actives.formulation: FDA-compliant OTC acne drug with exact exposure and directions
retail_target_actives.eligibility: no allergy, conflicting current active, or product contraindication

azelaic acid when clinically appropriate
care_path.review_period: Review response and tolerability at 12 weeks; improvement may not be visible for 6-8 weeks.
stop_or_escalate: severe irritation or allergic reaction
diagnostic uncertainty
painful deep lesions, scarring, or persistent pigment change
no adequate response after a completed reviewed course
prohibited_matches: unverified cosmetic retinol substituted for adapalene

labels.finding_wording: The image model found features most consistent with an inflamed papule (a small solid bump without visible pus).
labels.finding_limit: A photograph cannot confirm acne, measure depth, or exclude folliculitis, rosacea, perioral dermatitis, or another inflammatory eruption.

retail_target_actives.formulation: FDA-compliant OTC acne drug; exact reviewed path must state leave-on or short-contact exposure
retail_target_actives.cadence: per Drug Facts; alternate-day or short-contact introduction may be considered only in approved wording
retail_target_actives.eligibility: no allergy, conflicting current active, or product contraindication

retail_target_actives.formulation: FDA-labeled OTC leave-on acne drug, including an exact fixed benzoyl-peroxide combination when the label supports it
retail_target_actives.eligibility: age 12 or older; exclude pregnant or trying; defer nursing or unknown pregnancy status for clinician review

clinician_options: fixed topical combination therapy
azelaic acid
oral therapy for clinically assessed moderate-to-severe acne
care_path.review_period: Review response and tolerability at 12 weeks; improvement may not be visible for 6-8 weeks.
stop_or_escalate: rapid spread, fever, eye involvement, or severe pain
deep lesions or scarring
severe irritation or allergic reaction
no adequate response after a completed reviewed course
prohibited_matches: simultaneous topical and oral antibiotic without a clinician-managed regimen

labels.finding_wording: The image model found features most consistent with a pustule (an inflamed bump with visible pus).
labels.finding_limit: A photograph cannot confirm acne or exclude folliculitis, infection, rosacea, or another pustular eruption.

retail_target_actives.formulation: FDA-compliant OTC acne drug; exact reviewed path must state leave-on or short-contact exposure
retail_target_actives.cadence: per Drug Facts; alternate-day or short-contact introduction may be considered only in approved wording
retail_target_actives.eligibility: no allergy, conflicting current active, or product contraindication

retail_target_actives.eligibility: age 12 or older; exclude pregnant or trying; defer nursing or unknown pregnancy status for clinician review

clinician_options: fixed topical combination therapy
azelaic acid
oral therapy for clinically assessed moderate-to-severe acne
care_path.review_period: Review response and tolerability at 12 weeks; improvement may not be visible for 6-8 weeks.
stop_or_escalate: rapid spread, fever, eye involvement, or severe pain
deep lesions or scarring
severe irritation or allergic reaction
no adequate response after a completed reviewed course

labels.finding_wording: The image model found features most consistent with a nodule, but a photograph cannot establish that a lesion is deep or nodular.
labels.finding_limit: Depth, tenderness, fluctuation, cyst formation, and scarring risk require history and often palpation by a clinician.

clinician_options: confirm nodular or nodulocystic acne and assess severity and scarring risk
systemic therapy, including isotretinoin when clinically indicated
combination topical and systemic therapy
intralesional corticosteroid for selected large lesions
care_path.review_period: Prompt clinical assessment; urgency rises with severe pain, rapid worsening, systemic symptoms, or scarring.
stop_or_escalate: severe pain, rapid worsening, fever, or extensive involvement
scarring or psychosocial burden
diagnostic uncertainty

labels.finding_wording: The image model found features most consistent with a depressed acne scar.
labels.finding_limit: A photograph cannot reliably distinguish scar subtype, active inflammatory disease, enlarged pores, or other textural change, and it cannot assess skin mechanics.

clinician_options: control active acne before scar procedures
scar-subtype assessment
microneedling, laser, chemical reconstruction, filler, punch techniques, or surgery selected by a dermatologist
topical retinoid or salicylic acid for selected mild scarring only after clinical assessment
care_path.review_period: If severe scarring persists one year after acne clears, NICE recommends specialist referral; individual procedure follow-up is clinician-defined.
stop_or_escalate: active acne is uncontrolled
scar is new, changing, painful, bleeding, or diagnostically uncertain
procedure causes blistering, infection, worsening pigment change, or scarring
prohibited_matches: automatic retinoid or acid product match
silicone product substituted for a depressed-scar procedure path

labels.finding_wording: The image model found features most consistent with a raised scar.
labels.finding_limit: A photograph cannot confirm hypertrophic scar versus keloid, active inflammation, infection, tumor, or another raised lesion.

clinician_options: confirm hypertrophic scar versus keloid and whether the wound is fully closed
silicone sheet or silicone gel discussion, with explicit acknowledgement that comparative evidence is low certainty
injection, laser, cryotherapy, or surgery selected for the confirmed scar type
care_path.review_period: Silicone use, when recommended by a clinician, is commonly prolonged for months; exact duration and stop rules require reviewer-approved wording.
stop_or_escalate: open wound, infection, rash, skin breakdown, or worsening pain
rapid growth, bleeding, color change, or diagnostic uncertainty
prohibited_matches: automatic silicone product match without confirmed scar context and closed wound
generic dimethicone moisturizer substituted for dedicated scar-care evidence

labels.finding_wording: The image model found a pigment pattern that may be consistent with melasma.
labels.finding_limit: A photograph cannot confirm melasma or exclude post-inflammatory pigment change, medication-related pigmentation, a nevus, or skin cancer; dermoscopy, Wood lamp examination, or biopsy may be needed.

retail_target_actives.strength: ingredient identity required; no therapeutic percentage inferred
retail_target_actives.formulation: tinted broad-spectrum face sunscreen with SPF 30 or greater and exact SKU evidence
retail_target_actives.eligibility: no product contraindication or allergy; all sunscreen and iron-oxide evidence gates pass

clinician_options: hydroquinone prescription therapy
triple-combination prescription therapy
azelaic acid, kojic acid, or vitamin C in a clinician-selected plan
carefully selected procedure after diagnosis and pigment-risk assessment
care_path.review_period: Visible improvement often takes 3-12 months; reassess sooner if the pattern changes or diagnosis is uncertain.
stop_or_escalate: new, changing, asymmetric, bleeding, itching, or painful spot
irritation or paradoxical darkening
diagnostic uncertainty
history suggesting medication or hormonal trigger that needs clinician review
prohibited_matches: OTC hydroquinone or unapproved skin-lightening product
untinted sunscreen substituted when the reviewed path requires visible-light protection

labels.finding_wording: The image model found features most consistent with a mole (nevus), but it cannot determine that a spot is benign.
labels.finding_limit: A photograph and model score are not a skin-cancer screen. Dermoscopy, change over time, full history, and sometimes biopsy are needed.

clinician_options: dermatoscopic examination
biopsy or clinician removal when suspicious or bothersome
care_path.review_period: Seek prompt dermatology review for a new or evolving spot or any ABCDE feature; otherwise follow clinician-directed monitoring.
stop_or_escalate: asymmetry, irregular border, varied color, concerning diameter, or evolution
new mole after age 30
bleeding, itching, pain, rapid growth, or an outlier spot
prohibited_matches: acid, retinoid, freezing, burning, cutting, or home remedy
reassurance that the spot is benign

labels.finding_wording: The image model could not support one of the named lesion-specific pathways.
labels.finding_limit: The unsupported class can contain benign, infectious, inflammatory, medication-related, or malignant conditions that require different management.

clinician_options: history and examination to establish a diagnosis
care_path.review_period: No treatment review period is assigned because no treatment pathway is supported.
stop_or_escalate: new or changing lesion
bleeding, itching, pain, rapid spread, fever, eye involvement, or systemic symptoms
persistent concern or diagnostic uncertainty
prohibited_matches: substitution from the nearest detector label
reassurance or diagnosis based on model confidence
